title: Programmatic CLI integration and side-effects testing in Go
context: Testing command-line execution and state mutations (directory structures, git commands, and symlinks) programmatically in Go.
content: Structure Go CLI integration tests by utilizing isolated temporary directories (`t.TempDir()`), mock environment variables (e.g. `t.Setenv("QUORUM_SCHEMAS_DIR", ...)`), and programmatic executions of git commands (`exec.Command("git", ...)`). This isolates test side-effects, prevents leaks into the host file system, and ensures that the Go CLI code is structurally invocable and verified.
anti_patterns: Running integration tests directly on the host repository root directory, which can cause race conditions or uncommitted dirty file system states.
